core_payload.content:
# TileSims: The Ultimate Tiling Design Platform

## 1. Vision
TileSims transforms the way tilers, interior designers, and homeowners plan and visualize tiling projects. By combining precise LiDAR scanning with an intuitive, game-like interface (inspired by The Sims), it turns a complex technical task into an enjoyable creative experience. The platform bridges the gap between the physical space and digital design, enabling accurate material calculations, instant client presentations, and seamless collaboration.

## 2. Target Audience
- **Professional tilers** – reduce measurement errors, impress clients with real‑time 3D previews, generate accurate quotes.
- **Interior designers** – experiment with tile layouts, colors, and patterns without physical samples.
- **Homeowners (DIY)** – visualize renovation ideas, plan budgets, and share designs with contractors.
- **Tile retailers** – offer an interactive tool to help customers choose and order the right tiles.

## 3. Core Features
### 3.1 LiDAR‑Powered Scanning (iOS)
- Uses Apple’s RoomPlan API to capture a room’s geometry in under a minute.
- Generates a precise 3D model with walls, floors, doors, windows, and built‑in objects.
- Users can manually calibrate dimensions by entering a few reference measurements to achieve millimeter accuracy.

### 3.2 Blueprint / Scheme Integration
- Upload existing floor plans (PDF, PNG, DXF) and overlay them onto the scanned model.
- Manually align the scheme with the 3D scan using control points.
- Visualise discrepancies between the “as‑built” reality and the original plan.

### 3.3 Sims‑Like 3D Editor
- Drag‑and‑drop tiles from a rich catalog directly onto any surface.
- Real‑time preview with adjustable lighting (day/evening/night).
- Change layout patterns instantly: straight, diagonal, offset, herringbone, modular.
- Adjust joint width and grout colour with sliders.
- Click on any surface to select it and modify its tile assignment.

### 3.4 Tile Catalog
- Extensive library of real tiles from leading manufacturers (initially seeded with 200+ popular SKUs).
- Filter by size, colour, style, material, price.
- High‑quality PBR textures (diffuse, normal, roughness, metallic) for photorealistic rendering.
- Users can add custom tiles by uploading photos (future feature).

### 3.5 Smart Material Calculator
- Automatically computes the required number of tiles, including cuts and waste (5‑15% depending on complexity).
- Generates a detailed bill of materials with estimated cost.
- Exports a professional quote as PDF or shares it via a link.

### 3.6 Augmented Reality Preview (iOS/Android)
- Place the designed tiling onto the real room using ARKit/ARCore.
- Walk around and see how the chosen tiles look in the actual space before buying.

### 3.7 Generative AI (Future Roadmap)
- “Design assistant” chat powered by LLM (Claude/GPT) to suggest styles, colours, and layouts.
- “Magic wand” – select an area and let AI generate multiple tile pattern variations.
- Text‑to‑tile: describe a style (“modern Moroccan zellige with blue accents”) and get instant previews.

### 3.8 Collaboration & Sharing
- Projects are stored in the cloud and synchronised across iOS, web, and desktop.
- Share a public link with clients – they can view the 3D model, rotate, and approve without registration.
- Commenting and annotation tools for feedback.
- Role‑based access: owner, editor, viewer.

### 3.9 Community & Marketplace
- Public gallery of completed projects (anonymised) for inspiration.
- Like, save, and remix other users’ designs.
- Integration with tile suppliers for one‑click ordering (B2B).

## 4. Technical Architecture
- **Mobile (iOS)**: Swift, RoomPlan, ARKit, SceneKit – offline‑first, syncs with cloud.
- **Web (React + Three.js)**: Full editing suite, works on desktop and tablet.
- **Backend (Node.js + PostgreSQL + S3)**: REST API, file storage, user management.
- **Real‑time sync**: WebSocket for collaborative editing (future).
- **AI services**: Microservices for generative models, LLM integration (planned).

## 5. Alignment with CapsuleOS Philosophy
TileSims is a perfect embodiment of the “To Dig Deep” principle:
- Every scanned room becomes a **Room Capsule** (geometry + metadata).
- Major roadmap decisions are captured as linked capsules to preserve provenance and maintain a strict path Between Zero and One.
- Every design variation is a **Design Capsule** (linking room, tiles, layout parameters).
- The tile catalog consists of **Tile Capsules** (product data + textures).
- Projects aggregate multiple capsules into a **Project Capsule** (hub).
- Future AI suggestions will be generated via **N‑Infinity**, turning raw inspiration into structured capsules.

## 6. Development Roadmap
### Phase 1 – MVP (3‑4 months)
- iOS app with RoomPlan scanning, basic 3D viewer, one tile pattern (straight) on floor.
- Simple tile catalog (20 local tiles).
- Local storage of projects.
- Web viewer (read‑only) for shared links.

### Phase 2 – Core Editor (months 4‑6)
- Full web editor with all layout patterns.
- Cloud sync, user accounts.
- Blueprint upload and alignment.
- Material calculator with PDF export.

### Phase 3 – Advanced Features (months 6‑12)
- AR preview on iOS.
- Expanded tile catalog with manufacturer APIs.
- Generative AI integration.
- Community gallery and social features.
- B2B ordering integration.

## 7. Monetisation
- **Freemium**: Basic features free (limited projects, local tiles).
- **Pro subscription** (monthly/yearly): unlimited projects, full catalog, AI suggestions, advanced export.
- **Team plans** for tiling companies.
- **Marketplace commissions** from tile orders.
- **White‑label** for large retailers.

## 8. Conclusion
TileSims is not just another 3D design tool – it is a vertical solution crafted for the tiling industry, combining cutting‑edge technology with an addictive user experience. By making complex calculations invisible and design playfully intuitive, it empowers professionals to win more clients and complete projects faster, while delighting homeowners with a clear vision of their future space.

neuro_concentrate.summary: TileSims in the Dream branch captures the product’s intended leap from concept-rich simulation line into a more coherent spatial operating system. It carries the future state where scanning, layout logic, physical-object capsules, and branch-based renovation planning converge into one graph-native workflow. The branch matters because TileSims already has lineage and technical substance, but its strongest commercial and operational form still lives ahead. Dream therefore acts as the container for that fuller spatial product ambition.
